name: Pork Tenderloins with Orange Pepper Sauce
recipeIngredient: 12 ounces pork tenderloin, trimmed of fat
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the pork by cutting into 1/2-inch slices and pounding between 2 sheets of waxed paper until about 1/4 inch thick. Dust lightly with flour.
2. In a wide skillet, melt margarine over medium-high heat. Add the pork in stages if the pan isn't large enough, turning once to brown both sides thoroughly. Remove to a warmed serving platter and keep warm in a 200°F oven.
3. To the drippings, add shallots and pepper, cooking until shallots wilt. Then add wine, orange peel, and orange juice. Bring to boil, stirring frequently, then reduce to about 1/2 cup. Pour sauce over pork and serve.
